data.title: China seeks to boost Southeast Asian ties as Trump tariffs loom
data.description:
As Donald Trump's turbulent tariff moves rattle global markets, Chinese President Xi Jinping is expected to use his first overseas trip of the year to present China as a more stable trade partner.

Xi is visiting three Southeast Asian countries this week, starting with Vietnam on Monday, followed by Malaysia and Cambodia. Recall that once the 90-day pause on Trump's reciprocal tariffs ends, Vietnam is set to face a 46% tariff rate, Malaysia 24%, and Cambodia 49%.

"We should work together with the Global South to uphold the common interests of developing countries," Xi wrote in an article published in Nhan Dan (official newspaper of Vietnam's Communist Party).

"The trade war will produce no winner, and protectionism will lead nowhere. Our countries should resolutely safeguard the multilateral trading system, stable global industrial and supply chains, and open international environment," he added.

China is a major trading partner of Vietnam, Malaysia and Cambodia. But these countries are seeking exemptions from U.S. President Donald Trump's steep tariffs, for which his administration has laid out clear terms. 

"We will want to hear from countries including Cambodia, Mexico and Vietnam that you will stop allowing China to evade U.S. tariffs by trans-shipping exports through your countries," Trump's key adviser Peter Navarro wrote in a recent op-ed published in the Financial Times. 

To note, Vietnam and Malaysia have territorial disputes with Beijing in the South China Sea. China is unlikely to compromise on these disputes, but it may pledge more investment and trade to strengthen ties.

Cambodia, which has close ties with China and relies heavily on Chinese investment, is facing the highest tariff rate in Trump's reciprocal tariff list.

China also aims to reach out to the European Union as part of its diplomatic strategy to counter Trump's moves to isolate the country. The bloc's leaders are scheduled to meet with Xi in Beijing in July, at a time when the EU's ties with Washington are strained.

data.title: MP Materials surges as Trump plans order to stockpile deep-sea critical metals
data.description:
MP Materials (NYSE:MP) +14.3% pre-market Monday after the Financial Times reported that President Trump is set to issue an executive order allowing the stockpiling of critical metals from the Pacific Ocean seabed.

Trump already has invoked emergency powers to boost the ability of the U.S. to produce critical minerals as part of a broad effort to ramp up the development of domestic natural resources to reduce reliance on imports from China and elsewhere.

China is the world's biggest supplier of rare earth minerals, which comprise 17 elements in the periodic table, amounting to nearly 70% of the world's production of rare earths, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.

China has said it will tighten controls on exports of seven types of rare earths.

data.title: InMode expects Q1 sales below estimates
data.description:
• Israeli medical technology firm InMode (NASDAQ:INMD) expects revenue in the range of $77.2M to $77.5M for the first quarter of 2025, below analysts’ estimates of $82.21M.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin is estimated between 78% and 79%.
  • For full year 2025, the company expects revenue in the range of $395M to $405M, vs. $393.37M consensus.
  • InMode will host a conference call to discuss the first quarter 2025 financial results on Monday, April 28, 2025.
  • INMD -1.68% premarket to $14.63.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: R.J. O'Brien & Associates to merge with StoneX Group
S&P Global, CME Group to sell OSTTRA to KKR for $3.1B
data.description:
S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) and CME Group (NASDAQ:CME) on Monday announced a deal to sell OSTTRA to KKR-managed investment funds (NYSE:KKR).

The terms of the deal for OSTTRA equaled total enterprise value at $3.1B, subject to customary purchase price adjustments, which will be divided evenly between S&P Global and CME Group pursuant to their 50/50 joint venture.

The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2025.

Established in 2021 as a joint venture between CME Group and S&P Global, OSTTRA provides comprehensive suite of critical post-trade offerings across interest rates, FX, credit and equity asset classes. It offers end-to-end connectivity and workflow solutions to banks, broker-dealers, asset managers, and other market participants across trade processing, trade lifecycle, and optimization.

KKR is investing in OSTTRA primarily through its North American private equity strategy. Following the close of the transaction, the investment firm will support OSTTRA in creating a broad-based equity ownership program to provide all of the company's nearly 1,500 employees the opportunity to participate in the benefits of ownership.

SPGI +1.47% premarket to $471.99

Source: Press Release

data.title: Calibre delivers 71,539/oz in Q1
data.description:
• Calibre Mining (OTCQX:CXBMF) Monday reported consolidated gold production of 71,539 ounces; Nicaragua 64,469 ounces and Nevada 7,070 ounces.
  • The company's Valentine Gold Mine continues to advance, with first ore processed now expected in early Q3, followed by a steady ramp-up to nameplate capacity of 2.5 million tonnes.

data.title: Honey Badger Silver says CEO Dorian Nicol to step down
data.description:
• Honey Badger Silver (TSXV:TUF:CA) said Dorian Nicol will be stepping down as its CEO, effective today.
  • The firm added that Chairman Chad Williams will transition to executive chairman and interim CEO.

data.title: Nike partners with Kool-Aid for the first time with a Ja Morant basketball shoe
data.description:
Nike (NYSE:NKE) adding to its long history of deals with NBA stars with a new collaboration with Ja Morant and Kraft-Heinz's (NASDAQ:KHC) Kool-Aid brand. 

The new Nike x Kool-Aid Ja 2 was highlighted as being inspired by Memphis Grizzlies star Ja Morant's childhood memories and Kool-Aid's iconic flavors. Nike (NYSE:NKE) said the partnership merges sneaker culture, nostalgia, and creativity, reflecting Ja’s personality both on and off the court. The shoe design draws from Ja’s favorite Kool-Aid flavors, such as Blue Raspberry and Cherry, blending them into bold gradients alongside playful details like the Kool-Aid Man’s “Ohhh Yeah!” catchphrase and glow-in-the-dark outsoles. 

The collaboration also celebrates fans’ creativity in mixing flavors, which is a trend popularized on TikTok. 

The collection will feature an initial drop in May with performance-focused designs, and a second in July highlighting Kool-Aid's signature red colorway. To mark the launch, Kool-Aid is introducing a limited-edition flavor pack for fans to mix and match iconic flavors. Select fans can win custom Kool-Aid dubraes and laces to personalize their sneakers. 

Nike (NKE) has a long history of iconic shoe deals with NBA stars, including the iconic Michael Jordan Air Jordan line that revolutionized sneaker culture. Currently, LeBron James' signature shoes, Kevin Durant's KD series, and Giannis Antetokounmpo’s Zoom Freak line are all seeing strong sales trends.

Shares of Nike (NKE) were up 1.5% in premarket trading on Monday, amid a day of early gains for the stock market and companies tied to China manufacturing. Shares of Nike (NKE) are down 28.1% on a year-to-date basis.
